entries.summary: Ferdinand Marcos Jr. was sworn in as president of the Philippines.
Philippines accused a Chinese coast guard ship of using a laser against its crew.
Manila summoned China's ambassador to protest the laser incident at Second Thomas Shoal.
Chinese coast guard fired water cannons at a Philippine resupply boat at Second Thomas Shoal.
A Chinese coast guard vessel collided with a Philippine resupply boat near Second Thomas Shoal.
Chinese vessels used water cannons at Scarborough Shoal against Philippine fishing boats.
Manila said Chinese ships water-cannoned a resupply mission to Second Thomas Shoal.
Chinese coast guard injured Philippine sailors with water cannons during resupply attempt.
Filipino crew were hurt as Chinese vessels rammed and water-cannoned their resupply boat.
First trilateral US-Japan-Philippines summit at the White House addressed maritime tensions.
Manila said three Filipinos were injured in fresh Chinese water-cannon assault.
Chinese personnel boarded Philippine boats at Second Thomas Shoal, injuring a sailor.
Manila and Beijing announced a provisional Second Thomas Shoal resupply arrangement.
Philippine and Chinese coast guard ships collided near disputed Sabina Shoal.
China's coast guard claimed it took control measures against a Philippine vessel near Sabina Shoal.
China and the Philippines accused each other of incursions near the disputed Scarborough Shoal.
Marcos Jr. took office as Philippine president, signalling a recalibration toward Washington.
Philippines accused China's coast guard of pointing a military-grade laser at its crew.
Manila granted the United States access to four additional bases under the EDCA pact.
Chinese ships water-cannoned a Philippine boat resupplying troops on BRP Sierra Madre.
A Chinese coast guard vessel collided with a Philippine resupply ship at Second Thomas Shoal.
Filipino fishermen were targeted with water cannons near Scarborough Shoal.
Chinese vessels water-cannoned a Philippine boat carrying provisions to Second Thomas Shoal.
Manila said four Filipino sailors were injured in a Chinese water-cannon attack.
Chinese ships water-cannoned and damaged a Philippine boat in Second Thomas Shoal resupply.
Biden hosted Marcos and Kishida at first US-Japan-Philippines trilateral leaders' summit.
Chinese coast guard personnel armed with bladed weapons rammed Philippine boats at the shoal.
Manila and Beijing reached a provisional deal on Second Thomas Shoal resupplies.
Philippines successfully delivered supplies to BRP Sierra Madre under the new arrangement.
Coast guard ships from both countries collided near contested Sabina Shoal.
Manila said its coast guard would withdraw from Sabina Shoal after months at sea.
China and the Philippines accused each other of intrusions near the disputed Scarborough Shoal.
